name: error-diagnostics-error-analysis
description: You are an expert error analysis specialist with deep expertise in debugging distributed systems, analyzing production incidents, and implementing comprehensive observability solutions.
capabilities: Analyze error messages and stack traces to identify root causes
Apply systematic debugging techniques for distributed systems
Implement observability solutions with Sentry, DataDog, and OpenTelemetry
Create retry logic with exponential backoff for resilient error handling
Design monitoring and alerting strategies for production systems
Perform post-incident analysis and implement preventive measures
author: sickn33
securitySummary: After evaluating 108 static findings, all detections are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted markdown documentation patterns as security issues: backticks in code blocks were flagged as shell execution, example URLs were flagged as network exfiltration, and environment variable reads in example error-tracking code were flagged as credential access. The sensitive data deletion code (lines 751-752) is a security best practice that removes cookies/authorization headers before sending error reports. This is a legitimate error diagnostics skill providing observability documentation.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers quickly diagnose and resolve production errors using systematic debugging techniques, distributed tracing analysis, and comprehensive observability patterns.
limitations: Cannot directly access external error tracking systems without proper authentication
Requires error logs, traces, or stack traces as input to perform analysis
Cannot modify production systems - only provides guidance and recommendations
Effectiveness depends on quality of observability instrumentation in place

useCases.title: Investigate Production Incidents
useCases.description: Analyze production errors, correlate with deployments, and identify root cause using distributed tracing and log analysis.

useCases.title: Debug Application Errors
useCases.description: Examine stack traces, identify error patterns, and implement fixes for application-level errors.

useCases.title: Improve System Observability
useCases.description: Design and implement comprehensive error tracking, monitoring, and alerting solutions for better incident detection.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Error Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this error message and stack trace. Identify the likely cause and suggest a fix: $ERROR_MESSAGE
promptTemplates.scenario: When you encounter a specific error and need quick diagnostic guidance

promptTemplates.title: Distributed System Debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: Debug this distributed system error. The error occurred in service $SERVICE_NAME with trace ID $TRACE_ID. Examine the distributed trace and identify which upstream service caused the failure.
promptTemplates.scenario: When investigating errors in microservices architectures

promptTemplates.title: Post-Incident Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Conduct a post-incident review for this outage. Error pattern: $ERROR_PATTERN. Timeline: $TIMELINE. What were the contributing factors and what preventive measures would you recommend?
promptTemplates.scenario: After a production incident to identify systemic improvements

promptTemplates.title: Observability Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an observability implementation for a Node.js/Express application. Include error tracking setup with Sentry, distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry, and alerting rules for critical errors.
promptTemplates.scenario: When setting up comprehensive error monitoring from scratch

bestPractices: Always correlate errors with deployments, configuration changes, and external events
Implement structured logging with correlation IDs for distributed tracing
Create retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures
Establish error budgets and alerting thresholds based on user impact
antiPatterns: Ignoring intermittent errors - they often indicate systemic issues
Implementing generic error handling without context-specific recovery
Sending raw error data to external systems without scrubbing sensitive information
Setting alerting thresholds too low, causing alert fatigue

faq.question: What information should I provide for effective error analysis?
faq.answer: Provide the complete error message, full stack trace, timestamps, affected service names, recent deployment history, and any relevant log excerpts.

faq.question: How do I debug errors in a distributed system?
faq.answer: Use correlation IDs to trace requests across services, examine distributed tracing data, identify the failure point, and trace backward to find the root cause.

faq.question: What observability tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers Sentry, DataDog, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana, Jaeger, and other major observability platforms.

faq.question: Can this skill help implement error tracking?
faq.answer: Yes, the implementation playbook includes code samples for Sentry, DataDog, and other error tracking SDK integration.

faq.question: How do I prioritize errors for fixing?
faq.answer: Prioritize by user impact, error frequency, severity, and whether the error indicates a systemic issue or single-point failure.

faq.question: What is the recommended error handling strategy?
faq.answer: Implement defense in depth: retry logic with backoff, circuit breakers, proper error propagation, comprehensive logging, and automated alerting.
